items.title: If this session has no `tero__*` tools

items.title: 2) Register (user scope → ~/.grok/config.toml)
items.summary: grok mcp add tero \

items.title: 3) Verify, then new session (or /mcps → r if already inside TUI)
items.summary: grok mcp doctor tero   # handshake OK, 9 tools

items.summary: This document tracks the phased development of cabal-devmelopner.

items.title: PoC Phase (Current)
items.summary: Goal: Validate core architecture and get a working, extensible agent.

items.title: Deliverables
items.summary: - [x] Project skeleton + installable CLI

items.title: MVP Phase
items.summary: Goal: Make cabal-devmelopner a practical daily co-dev tool.

items.title: Key Features
items.summary: - Full-featured TUI (status, progress, logs, task management)

items.title: Exit Criteria
items.summary: - Comfortable to use for real development work.

items.title: Production Phase
items.summary: Goal: Robust, scalable, multi-agent development system.

items.title: Key Features
items.summary: - Multi-provider support (xAI, Claude, others)

items.title: Exit Criteria
items.summary: - Can reliably run long development sessions with minimal human intervention.

items.title: Notes
items.summary: - All phases should maintain the core principles: repo-agnostic, extensible communications, strong Tero integration, and clean architecture.

items.title: cabal-devmelopner — 1.0.0 Gap Analysis
items.summary: Baseline: dev @ 5fd9781 (fleet harden) + product tree ~2.0 kLOC under src/cabaldevmelopner/

items.title: 1. What 1.0.0 means (product definition)
items.summary: 1.0.0 = daily co-dev agent you can trust on a real repo without hand-copying model output.

items.title: Execution order (maintainer direction 2026-07-21)
items.summary: 1. Tools usable — write/applypatch + multi-step loop + verify (E1 → E2)

items.title: 4. Epics for 1.0.0 (GitHub milestone `v1.0.0`)
items.summary: Outcome: Versioning, CI gates, docs SoT, milestone hygiene.

items.title: Epic E0 — Release foundation (Ship)
items.summary: Outcome: Versioning, CI gates, docs SoT, milestone hygiene.

items.title: Epic E1 — Act on codebases (Tools v1)
items.summary: Outcome: Agent can modify a repo safely.

items.title: Epic E2 — Verify loop
items.summary: Outcome: Edits are checked, not trusted.

items.title: Epic E3 — Config, budgets, packaging
items.summary: Outcome: Installable, tunable leaf.

items.title: Epic E4 — Providers & streaming
items.summary: Outcome: Local default, frontier optional, progressive UX.

items.title: Epic E5 — Tero & memory honesty
items.summary: Outcome: Opt-in context that never lies.

items.title: Epic E6 — TUI v1
items.summary: Outcome: Daily co-dev surface.

items.title: Epic E7 — Notify + HITL
items.summary: Outcome: Async awareness + approval.

items.title: Epic E8 — Hardening for 1.0 ship
items.summary: Outcome: Trust.

items.title: 5. Suggested issue graph (disjoint by design)
items.summary: flowchart TB

items.title: 6. Definition of Done for 1.0.0
items.summary: - [ ] All P0 issues under E0–E8 closed

items.title: 8. Open decisions (need deliberation)
items.summary: 1. Write tool shape: full file write vs unified diff apply only? (Recommend diff/apply first for safer review.)

items.title: 9. Bottom line
items.summary: Today (0.1.0): honest config + tools-read scaffold + dual provider + Tero opt-in — a strong architecture alpha, not yet a 1.0 development agent.
